scope: Top 3 high-yield tests: smoke v2.1.1, tester escalation, worker restart resume

tests.name: smoke v2.1.1

vs_v2_0_baseline_gs_uud.caveat: different story shapes (gs-uud was 200 LOC list-prs; gs-figi was 30-50 LOC --version flag); per-LOC time is not strictly comparable

findings_surfaced: gh-stats .gitignore excludes plans/ and reviews/ → reviewer recorded reviewer.no_commit_review_gitignore_conflict=true; audit trail lost. Fixed in v2.1.2 via git add -f.

tests.name: tester anti-weakening escalation (contradiction detection)
tests.story_summary: --canonical flag with deliberately contradictory acceptance criteria (sort alphabetically AND preserve header order)
tests.expected_path: (a) worker detects contradiction in plan step and escalates
tests.actual_path: (a) worker detected contradiction in plan step and escalated
worker_did: Read bead description and rig CLAUDE.md
Wrote a structured plan documenting the contradiction with concrete counter-example (CSV header name,age,id vs sorted age,id,name)
Considered three creative reinterpretations and rejected each with reasoning
Identified secondary scope error: csv2json/writer.py named in story Scope but does not exist on this rig
Proposed three concrete resolution paths (R1/R2/R3) for the story author to pick from
Mailed witness/mayor via gc mail send
worker_did_not: Create the per-bead worktree or feature branch (workspace-setup never reached)
findings_surfaced: Worker used status=blocked despite worker prompt explicitly saying --status=escalated. Small prompt-clarity inconsistency. Tracked under v2.2 audit findings.
Plan file written before workspace-setup lives in the agent's pre_start work_dir, not a per-bead worktree. Confirms plan-file scattering finding.

tests.name: worker restart resume mid-chain
tests.story_summary: --count-only flag (small story used as test vehicle for restart-resume)
tests.expected_path: monitor triggers gc session reset on first implement-step observation; supervisor respawns fresh worker; new worker resumes from metadata.current_step
tests.actual_path: monitor's 60s buffer was longer than the worker's remaining implement work; worker.completed_at=22:26:46 fired ~55s before the reset trigger at 22:27:41. The reset signal hit an already-drained session; the chain continued normally with no interruption observed.
what_was_validated: Chain is robust to a stray gc session reset signal mid-flight (no break, no stall)
Supervisor accepted and processed the reset cleanly
what_was_not_validated: The documented gc runtime request-restart → supervisor respawn → resume-from-current_step path
tests.design_flaw_for_retest: implement step on small stories (3-5 min) is too short for a 60s observation-then-trigger monitor. Either: (a) use a larger story (15+ min implement); (b) trigger within 30s of first implement observation; (c) accept the chain-robustness signal and move on.
incidental_observations: All four pre-finalizer phases (worker, tester, reviewer, documenter) recorded the same session_id bl-mz3eot. The supervisor reused a single pool instance across phases on this lightly-loaded workspace. Only the finalizer got a separate session (bl-cbl9f0). Emergent behavior of low-demand pools, not a bug; tracked for the pool sizing audit.

phase5_summary.ship_signal: Pack-level architecture is sound. v2.1.x is in usable shape. Remaining backlog is bug-tier and concurrency-tier work, not architectural redesign.
